info.title: Strimulator API
info.description: Local Stripe emulator for development and testing. Strimulator faithfully emulates Stripe's REST API, letting you build and test payment integrations without hitting the real Stripe servers.

tags.name: Customers
tags.description: Customer objects allow you to perform recurring charges and track payments. Strimulator emulates Stripe's customer API.

tags.name: Products
tags.description: Products describe the items or services you offer to your customers.

tags.name: Prices
tags.description: Prices define the unit cost, currency, and billing cycle for both one-time and recurring purchases of products.

tags.name: Payment Methods
tags.description: Payment method objects represent a customer's payment instruments, such as credit cards. Strimulator supports the card type with magic test tokens.

tags.name: Payment Intents
tags.description: A PaymentIntent guides you through the process of collecting a payment from your customer. Strimulator emulates the full lifecycle including 3DS simulation.

tags.name: Setup Intents
tags.description: A SetupIntent guides you through the process of setting up a customer's payment credentials for future payments.

tags.name: Charges
tags.description: Charge objects are created automatically when a PaymentIntent succeeds. They represent the movement of money.

tags.name: Refunds
tags.description: Refund objects allow you to refund a charge that has been previously created.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Subscriptions allow you to charge a customer on a recurring basis. Strimulator supports trial periods, plan changes, and cancellation.

tags.name: Invoices
tags.description: Invoices are statements of amounts owed by a customer. They are automatically created by subscriptions and can be finalized, paid, or voided.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Events are Strimulator's way of letting you know when something interesting happens. Events are created for every state change in the system.

tags.name: Webhook Endpoints
tags.description: Configure webhook endpoints to receive event notifications via HTTP POST. Strimulator delivers webhooks with HMAC-SHA256 signatures.

tags.name: Test Clocks
tags.description: Test clocks allow you to simulate the passage of time for testing subscription billing, trial expirations, and other time-sensitive features.

post.description: Creates a new customer object.

name.description: The customer's full name or business name.

description.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of customers, sorted by creation date (most recent first).

200.description: A paginated list of customer objects.

get.description: Search for customers using Stripe's search query language. Supports filtering by `email`, `name`, `metadata`, and other fields.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string using Stripe search syntax (e.g., `email:"user@example.com"`).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100, default 10).

200.description: A search result containing matching customers.

get.description: Retrieves a customer object by its ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the customer to retrieve (e.g., `cus_...`).

post.description: Updates the specified customer by setting the values of the parameters passed. Any parameters not provided will be left unchanged.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the customer to update.

name.description: The customer's full name or business name.

description.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a customer. It cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the customer to delete.

id.description: The ID of the deleted customer.

post.description: Creates a new product object.

name.description: The product's name, displayed to customers.

active.description: Whether the product is currently available for purchase. Defaults to `true`.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

statement_descriptor.description: Descriptor for the product, shown on customer statements.

unit_label.description: A label that represents units of this product.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of products.

200.description: A paginated list of product objects.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing product.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the product to retrieve (e.g., `prod_...`).

post.description: Updates the specified product by setting the values of the parameters passed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the product to update.

active.description: Whether the product is available for purchase.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

unit_label.description: A label that represents units of this product.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the product to delete.

post.description: Creates a new price for an existing product. The price can be one-time or recurring.

unit_amount.description: A positive integer in cents representing how much to charge.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase (e.g., `usd`).

product.description: The ID of the product this price belongs to (e.g., `prod_...`).

recurring.description: The recurring components of a price such as `interval` and `interval_count`.

interval.description: The frequency at which a subscription is billed.

interval_count.description: The number of intervals between subscription billings. Defaults to 1.

active.description: Whether the price is currently active. Defaults to `true`.

nickname.description: A brief description of the price, hidden from customers.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

lookup_key.description: A lookup key used to retrieve prices dynamically.

tax_behavior.description: Specifies whether the price is inclusive or exclusive of tax.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of prices. Optionally filter by product.

parameters.name: product
parameters.description: Only return prices belonging to this product ID.

200.description: A paginated list of price objects.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing price.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the price to retrieve (e.g., `price_...`).

post.description: Updates the specified price. Only mutable fields like `active`, `nickname`, and `metadata` can be changed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the price to update.

active.description: Whether the price is currently active.

nickname.description: A brief description of the price.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

lookup_key.description: A lookup key used to retrieve prices dynamically.

tax_behavior.description: Specifies whether the price is inclusive or exclusive of tax.

post.description: Creates a payment method object. Strimulator supports `card` type with magic test tokens like `tok_visa`, `tok_mastercard`, `tok_amex`, `tok_visa_debit`, `tok_threeDSecureRequired`, and `tok_threeDSecureOptional`.

type.description: The type of payment method. Only `card` is supported.

card.description: Card details. Provide a `token` for test cards.

exp_month.description: Two-digit number representing the card's expiration month.

exp_year.description: Four-digit number representing the card's expiration year.

billing_details.description: Billing information associated with the payment method.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

get.description: Returns a list of payment methods for a given customer.

parameters.name: customer
parameters.description: The ID of the customer whose payment methods will be listed.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of payment methods to list. Defaults to `card`.

200.description: A paginated list of payment method objects.

get.description: Retrieves a payment method object.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the payment method to retrieve (e.g., `pm_...`).

post.summary: Attach a payment method to a customer
post.description: Attaches a payment method object to a customer.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the payment method to attach.

customer.description: The ID of the customer to attach the payment method to.

post.summary: Detach a payment method from its customer
post.description: Detaches a payment method object from the customer it is attached to. The payment method still exists but can no longer be used for payments.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the payment method to detach.

200.description: The detached payment method object (customer field set to null).

post.description: Creates a PaymentIntent object. Optionally set `confirm=true` with a `payment_method` to create and confirm in one step.

amount.description: Amount intended to be collected, in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents).

currency.description: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

customer.description: ID of the customer this PaymentIntent is for.

payment_method.description: ID of the payment method to use.

confirm.description: Set to `true` to attempt to confirm this PaymentIntent immediately.

capture_method.description: Controls when charges will be captured. Defaults to `automatic`.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of PaymentIntents. Optionally filter by customer.

parameters.name: customer
parameters.description: Only return PaymentIntents for this customer ID.

200.description: A paginated list of PaymentIntent objects.

get.description: Search for PaymentIntents using Stripe's search query language.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string (e.g., `status:"succeeded"`).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100, default 10).

200.description: A search result containing matching PaymentIntents.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a PaymentIntent. Supports expansion of `customer`, `payment_method`, and `latest_charge`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the PaymentIntent to retrieve (e.g., `pi_...`).

post.description: Updates properties on a PaymentIntent without confirming. Returns the current PaymentIntent.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the PaymentIntent to update.

post.description: Confirms a PaymentIntent, transitioning it to `succeeded`, `requires_capture`, or `requires_action` (for 3DS). Payment methods with last4 `3220` (`tok_threeDSecureRequired`) trigger 3DS simulation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the PaymentIntent to confirm.

payment_method.description: ID of the payment method to use for this PaymentIntent.

capture_method.description: Controls when charges will be captured.

post.description: Captures a PaymentIntent that has a status of `requires_capture`. This is used when `capture_method=manual`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the PaymentIntent to capture.

amount_to_capture.description: The amount to capture, which must be less than or equal to the original amount. Defaults to the full amount.

400.description: Invalid state transition (e.g., not in `requires_capture` status).

post.description: Cancels a PaymentIntent. You cannot cancel a PaymentIntent with a status of `succeeded`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the PaymentIntent to cancel.

post.description: Creates a SetupIntent object for setting up a customer's payment method for future payments.

customer.description: ID of the customer this SetupIntent is for.

payment_method.description: ID of the payment method to attach.

confirm.description: Set to `true` to attempt to confirm this SetupIntent immediately.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of SetupIntents.

200.description: A paginated list of SetupIntent objects.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a SetupIntent.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the SetupIntent to retrieve (e.g., `seti_...`).

post.description: Confirms a SetupIntent, transitioning it to `succeeded`. If a `payment_method` is provided, it attaches the payment method to the customer.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the SetupIntent to confirm.

payment_method.description: ID of the payment method to use for this SetupIntent.

post.description: Cancels a SetupIntent. You cannot cancel a SetupIntent that has already succeeded.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the SetupIntent to cancel.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of charges. Optionally filter by payment intent.

parameters.name: payment_intent
parameters.description: Only return charges for this PaymentIntent ID.

200.description: A paginated list of charge objects.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a charge. Supports expansion of `customer` and `payment_intent`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the charge to retrieve (e.g., `ch_...`).

post.description: Creates a refund for a charge or payment intent. You must provide either `charge` or `payment_intent`.

charge.description: The ID of the charge to refund.

payment_intent.description: The ID of the PaymentIntent to refund.

amount.description: A positive integer in cents representing how much to refund. Defaults to the full charge amount.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g., missing both charge and payment_intent).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of refunds. Optionally filter by charge or payment intent.

parameters.name: charge
parameters.description: Only return refunds for this charge ID.

parameters.name: payment_intent
parameters.description: Only return refunds for this PaymentIntent ID.

200.description: A paginated list of refund objects.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a refund.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the refund to retrieve (e.g., `re_...`).

post.description: Creates a new subscription on an existing customer. Each subscription must have at least one item with a recurring price.

customer.description: The ID of the customer to subscribe.

items.description: A list of prices and quantities the customer is subscribed to. Use bracket notation: `items[0][price]=price_...&items[0][quantity]=1`.

price.description: The ID of the price to subscribe to.

quantity.description: The quantity of the plan. Defaults to 1.

trial_period_days.description: Number of trial days before charging the customer. Sets the subscription to `trialing` status.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

test_clock.description: ID of the test clock to associate with this subscription.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of subscriptions. Optionally filter by customer.

parameters.name: customer
parameters.description: Only return subscriptions for this customer ID.

200.description: A paginated list of subscription objects.

get.description: Search for subscriptions using Stripe's search query language.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string (e.g., `status:"active"`).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100, default 10).

200.description: A search result containing matching subscriptions.

get.description: Retrieves the subscription with the given ID. Supports expansion of `customer` and `latest_invoice` (including nested `latest_invoice.payment_intent`).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the subscription to retrieve (e.g., `sub_...`).

post.description: Updates an existing subscription. You can change the plan, quantity, trial end, and cancellation settings.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the subscription to update.

items.description: A list of subscription items, each with a price and optional quantity.

id.description: The ID of the subscription item to update.

price.description: The ID of the price to switch to.

cancel_at_period_end.description: If set to `true`, the subscription will be canceled at the end of the current period.

trial_end.description: Unix timestamp or the string `now`. Sets the trial end date.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

delete.description: Cancels a customer's subscription immediately. The subscription status transitions to `canceled`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the subscription to cancel.

post.description: Creates a new invoice for a customer. Invoices are created as drafts and must be finalized before they can be paid.

customer.description: The ID of the customer to create the invoice for.

subscription.description: The ID of the subscription to create the invoice for.

currency.description: Three-letter ISO currency code. Defaults to `usd`.

amount_due.description: The amount due on the invoice, in cents.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of invoices. Optionally filter by customer or subscription.

parameters.name: customer
parameters.description: Only return invoices for this customer ID.

parameters.name: subscription
parameters.description: Only return invoices for this subscription ID.

200.description: A paginated list of invoice objects.

get.description: Search for invoices using Stripe's search query language.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query string (e.g., `customer:"cus_..."`).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-100, default 10).

200.description: A search result containing matching invoices.

get.description: Retrieves the invoice with the given ID. Supports expansion of `customer`, `subscription`, and `payment_intent`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the invoice to retrieve (e.g., `in_...`).

post.description: Finalizes a draft invoice, transitioning it to `open` status and assigning an invoice number. Once finalized, the invoice can be paid.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the invoice to finalize.

400.description: Invalid state transition (e.g., invoice is not a draft).

post.description: Pays an open invoice, transitioning it to `paid` status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the invoice to pay.

400.description: Invalid state transition (e.g., invoice is not open).

post.description: Voids an open invoice, transitioning it to `void` status. A voided invoice can no longer be paid.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the invoice to void.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of events, sorted by creation date (most recent first). Optionally filter by event type.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter events by type (e.g., `customer.created`, `payment_intent.succeeded`).

200.description: A paginated list of event objects.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an event.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the event to retrieve (e.g., `evt_...`).

post.description: Creates a webhook endpoint that listens to specific events. Strimulator will POST event payloads to the configured URL with HMAC-SHA256 signatures.

url.description: The URL of the webhook endpoint.

enabled_events.description: The list of event types to listen for (e.g., `["customer.created", "payment_intent.succeeded"]`). Use `["*"]` to listen to all events.

description.description: An optional description of what the webhook is used for.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs for storing additional information.

200.description: The created webhook endpoint object. The response includes the webhook `secret` needed for signature verification.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g., missing url or enabled_events).

get.description: Returns a paginated list of webhook endpoints.

200.description: A paginated list of webhook endpoint objects.

get.description: Retrieves the webhook endpoint with the given ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook endpoint to retrieve (e.g., `we_...`).

delete.description: Deletes a webhook endpoint. Strimulator will stop sending events to this URL.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the webhook endpoint to delete.

post.description: Creates a new test clock with the specified frozen time. Use test clocks to simulate the passage of time for subscription billing and trial expirations.

frozen_time.description: The initial frozen time as a Unix timestamp.

name.description: A human-readable name for the test clock.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of test clocks.

200.description: A paginated list of test clock objects.

get.description: Retrieves a test clock.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the test clock to retrieve (e.g., `clock_...`).

delete.description: Deletes a test clock and all associated resources.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the test clock to delete.

post.description: Advances a test clock's frozen time forward. This triggers billing cycle processing for any linked subscriptions, including rolling periods, creating invoices, and processing payments.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the test clock to advance.

frozen_time.description: The new frozen time as a Unix timestamp. Must be greater than the current frozen time.

200.description: The updated test clock object with the new frozen time.

400.description: Invalid request (e.g., frozen_time is not in the future).

BearerAuth.description: Authenticate with a test API key. Use any string starting with `sk_test_` (e.g., `sk_test_12345`).

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned, between 1 and 100. Default is 10.

StartingAfter.name: starting_after
StartingAfter.description: A cursor for pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. Retrieves the next page of results after this object.

EndingBefore.name: ending_before
EndingBefore.description: A cursor for pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. Retrieves the previous page of results before this object.

Expand.name: expand[]
Expand.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded. Supports dot notation for nested expansion (e.g., `latest_invoice.payment_intent`).

Error.description: An error response from the API.

message.description: A human-readable message providing more details about the error.

code.description: For some errors (e.g., `resource_missing`, `payment_intent_unexpected_state`), a short string indicating the error code.

param.description: If the error is parameter-specific, the parameter related to the error.

decline_code.description: For card errors, the decline code from the issuer.

ListResponse.description: A list of objects with cursor-based pagination.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Always `list`.

data.description: An array of the requested objects.

has_more.description: Whether there are more results available after this set.

url.description: The URL where this list can be accessed.

SearchResult.description: A search result containing matching objects.

object.description: String representing the object's type. Always `search_result`.

data.description: An array of the matching objects.

has_more.description: Whether there are more results available.

total_count.description: The total number of objects that match the query.

url.description: The URL where this search can be accessed.

next_page.description: A cursor for the next page of results. Always `null` in Strimulator.

Customer.description: A customer object represents a customer of your business.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

currency.description: The currency the customer can be charged in.

default_source.description: ID of the default payment source.

delinquent.description: Whether the customer currently has an unpaid invoice.

description.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object.

discount.description: Discount object currently applied to the customer.

invoice_prefix.description: The prefix for the customer used to generate unique invoice numbers.

invoice_settings.description: Default invoice settings for the customer.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

name.description: The customer's full name or business name.

test_clock.description: ID of the test clock this customer belongs to.

Product.description: A product represents an item or service you sell.

active.description: Whether the product is currently available for purchase.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

default_price.description: The ID of the default price for this product.

images.description: A list of image URLs for this product.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

name.description: The product's name, displayed to customers.

type.description: The type of the product. Always `service` in Strimulator.

unit_label.description: A label that represents units of this product.

updated.description: Time at which the object was last updated (Unix timestamp).

Price.description: A price defines the unit cost, currency, and billing cycle for a product.

active.description: Whether the price is currently active.

billing_scheme.description: Describes how to compute the price per period.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

currency.description: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

lookup_key.description: A lookup key used to retrieve prices dynamically.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

nickname.description: A brief description of the price.

product.description: The ID of the product this price is for.

recurring.description: The recurring components of a price. Only present for recurring prices.

interval_count.description: The number of intervals between billings.

usage_type.description: How the quantity per period is determined.

type.description: Whether the price is one-time or recurring.

unit_amount_decimal.description: The unit amount in cents as a decimal string.

PaymentMethod.description: A payment method represents a customer's payment instrument (e.g., a credit card).

billing_details.description: Billing information associated with the payment method.

country.description: Two-letter ISO code for the card's issuing country.

display_brand.description: The display brand of the card.

exp_month.description: Two-digit number representing the card's expiration month.

exp_year.description: Four-digit number representing the card's expiration year.

fingerprint.description: Uniquely identifies this particular card number.

last4.description: The last four digits of the card.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

customer.description: The ID of the customer to which this payment method is attached, or `null`.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

type.description: The type of the payment method. Only `card` is supported.

PaymentIntent.description: A PaymentIntent guides you through the process of collecting a payment from your customer.

amount.description: Amount intended to be collected, in cents.

amount_capturable.description: Amount that can be captured from this PaymentIntent.

canceled_at.description: Time at which the PaymentIntent was canceled (Unix timestamp).

capture_method.description: Controls when the funds will be captured.

client_secret.description: The client secret for this PaymentIntent, used in client-side integrations.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

currency.description: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

customer.description: ID of the customer this PaymentIntent is for.

description.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object.

last_payment_error.description: The payment error encountered in the previous payment attempt.

latest_charge.description: The ID of the most recent charge created by this PaymentIntent.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

next_action.description: If present, this property tells you what actions you need to take for the customer to continue the payment (e.g., 3DS authentication).

payment_method.description: ID of the payment method used in this PaymentIntent.

payment_method_types.description: The list of payment method types that this PaymentIntent can use.

SetupIntent.description: A SetupIntent guides you through the process of setting up a customer's payment credentials for future payments.

client_secret.description: The client secret for this SetupIntent.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

customer.description: ID of the customer this SetupIntent is for.

last_setup_error.description: The error encountered in the previous setup attempt.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

payment_method.description: ID of the payment method attached to this SetupIntent.

payment_method_types.description: The list of payment method types supported.

usage.description: Indicates the future usage of this SetupIntent's payment method.

Charge.description: A charge represents a payment of money. Charges are created automatically when a PaymentIntent succeeds.

billing_details.description: Billing information associated with the payment method.

captured.description: Whether the charge has been captured.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

customer.description: ID of the customer this charge is for.

disputed.description: Whether the charge has been disputed.

failure_code.description: Error code explaining reason for charge failure.

invoice.description: ID of the invoice this charge is for.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

payment_intent.description: ID of the PaymentIntent that created this charge.

payment_method.description: ID of the payment method used.

refunded.description: Whether the charge has been fully refunded.

refunds.description: A list of refunds that have been applied to the charge.

Refund.description: A refund represents the return of funds to a customer.

charge.description: ID of the charge that was refunded.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

payment_intent.description: ID of the PaymentIntent that was refunded.

Subscription.description: A subscription allows you to charge a customer on a recurring basis.

billing_cycle_anchor.description: Determines the date of the first full invoice.

cancel_at.description: Time at which the subscription will be automatically canceled (Unix timestamp).

cancel_at_period_end.description: Whether the subscription will be canceled at the end of the current period.

canceled_at.description: Time at which the subscription was canceled (Unix timestamp).

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

current_period_end.description: End of the current period (Unix timestamp).

current_period_start.description: Start of the current period (Unix timestamp).

customer.description: ID of the customer who owns the subscription.

default_payment_method.description: ID of the default payment method.

ended_at.description: Time at which the subscription ended (Unix timestamp).

items.description: List of subscription items, each with an assigned price.

latest_invoice.description: The most recent invoice this subscription has generated.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

test_clock.description: ID of the test clock this subscription is associated with.

trial_end.description: End of the trial period (Unix timestamp).

trial_start.description: Start of the trial period (Unix timestamp).

SubscriptionItem.description: A subscription item represents a single line item in a subscription.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

quantity.description: The quantity of the plan to which the customer is subscribed.

subscription.description: The ID of the subscription this item belongs to.

Invoice.description: An invoice is a statement of amounts owed by a customer.

amount_due.description: Final amount due at this time for the invoice, in cents.

amount_paid.description: The amount that has been paid, in cents.

amount_remaining.description: The amount remaining to be paid, in cents.

attempt_count.description: Number of payment attempts made for this invoice.

attempted.description: Whether an attempt has been made to pay the invoice.

auto_advance.description: Controls whether Stripe performs automatic collection.

billing_reason.description: Indicates the reason why the invoice was created.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

customer.description: The ID of the customer who will be billed.

effective_at.description: Time at which the invoice was finalized (Unix timestamp).

lines.description: The individual line items that make up the invoice.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

paid.description: Whether the invoice has been paid.

payment_intent.description: The PaymentIntent associated with this invoice.

period_end.description: End of the usage period the invoice covers (Unix timestamp).

period_start.description: Start of the usage period the invoice covers (Unix timestamp).

subscription.description: The subscription that this invoice was prepared for.

subtotal.description: Total of all subscriptions, invoice items, and prorations before discount or tax.

Event.description: Events are Strimulator's way of letting you know when something interesting happens in your account.

api_version.description: The Stripe API version used to render `data`.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

object.description: The object that triggered the event.

previous_attributes.description: The values of attributes that have changed (only included for `*.updated` events).

pending_webhooks.description: Number of webhooks that have yet to be successfully delivered.

request.description: Information on the API request that triggered the event.

WebhookEndpoint.description: A webhook endpoint receives event notifications via HTTP POST.

api_version.description: The API version events are rendered as.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

enabled_events.description: The list of events to enable for this endpoint. Use `["*"]` for all events.

metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs attached to the object.

secret.description: The endpoint's secret, used to generate webhook signatures. Only returned on creation.

status.description: The status of the webhook endpoint.

url.description: The URL of the webhook endpoint.

TestClock.description: A test clock enables deterministic control over objects in testmode by simulating the passage of time.

created.description: Time at which the object was created (Unix timestamp).

deletes_after.description: Time at which this test clock will be automatically deleted (Unix timestamp).

frozen_time.description: The frozen time for this test clock (Unix timestamp).

name.description: A human-readable name for the test clock.

status.description: The status of the test clock.
